content.problemStatement:
There are NNN equi-spaced points numbered 1,2,…,N1, 2, \ldots, N1,2,…,N. Initially, all points are unmarked.

In one operation, you can choose XXX such that 1<X<N1 < X < N1<X<N, and then mark points X−1,X,X+1X - 1, X, X + 1X−1,X,X+1. (If any of them were already marked, they do not get affected)

Finally, you will make a binary string SSS, where Si=1S_i = 1Si​=1 if and only if you marked point iii, and otherwise Si=0S_i = 0Si​=0.

Now, you forgot what operations you did. You have a binary string SSS with you. You want to check if it is possible to obtain SSS by doing some of the above operations of marking points.

Print Yes\text{Yes}Yes if it is possible to obtain SSS and No\text{No}No otherwise.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the number of points.
The second line contains SSS - a binary string.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line Yes\text{Yes}Yes if it is possible to obtain SSS and No\text{No}No otherwise.

sampleTestCases.output:

Yes

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : You can choose to not mark anything.
Test Case 2 : First perform one operation with X=2X = 2X=2 and then with X=3X = 3X=3.
